issues.title: [ARM64 / Qualcomm Adreno 680] GPU acceleration unavailable in WSL2 — /dev/dri/renderD128 not created by dxgkrnl, Mesa falls back to llvmpipe
issues.next_action_reason: ARM64 Qualcomm Adreno GPU acceleration unsupported (no /dev/dri/renderD128). Long unanswered (32d). Set expectations on Adreno WDDM/GPU-PV support + label GPU.
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks @dorepi and @Gerdya. On ARM64 Snapdragon devices, GPU-PV/`/dev/dri/renderD128` requires a WDDM driver that exposes the DirectX 12 paravirtualized GPU to the guest; current Adreno 680/685 drivers don't, so Mesa falls back to llvmpipe. Could you share your exact Adreno driver version and `dxdiag` WDDM level? We'll label this `GPU` and track Adreno support, but note the enabling piece is the Windows-side GPU driver rather than WSLg.

issues.title: WSLg 1.0.71: GUI session freezes, then Weston becomes unkillable in /mnt/shared_memory virtiofs
issues.next_action_reason: Well-written hang report (weston unkillable, virtiofs shared_memory) with zero replies for 12d. Acknowledge + request logs/repro to move it forward.
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks for the thorough write-up @Mygod, and sorry for the silence. A weston process that hangs and becomes unkillable while blocked on `/mnt/shared_memory` virtiofs points at a shared-memory/virtiofs stall. Could you attach `wsl --version`, a `cat /proc/<weston-pid>/stack` (or `wchan`) while it's wedged, and a `WslLogs` capture around the freeze? That kernel stack will tell us whether it's stuck in the virtiofs path.
